---
title: Basic Workflow
jupyter:
  jupytext:
    text_representation:
      extension: .qmd
      format_name: quarto
      format_version: '1.0'
      jupytext_version: 1.19.1
  kernelspec:
    display_name: renewal_model_dj
    language: python
    name: renewal_model_dj
---

```{python}
from __future__ import annotations

import json
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

import arviz as az
from arviz_base import from_cmdstanpy
import numpy as np
import plotnine as p9
import polars as pl
import pandas as pd
from cmdstanpy import CmdStanMCMC, CmdStanModel, format_stan_file

np.set_printoptions(suppress=True)
pd.set_option('display.float_format', '{:.4f}'.format)
```

```{python}
def plot_rt_posterior(
    rt,
    true_rt=None,
    interval: float = 0.90,
    title: str = "Posterior Rt with 90% interval",
):
    alpha = (1 - interval) / 2
    q_low = alpha
    q_high = 1 - alpha

    time = rt.coords["time"].values

    rt_df = pd.DataFrame(
        {
            "day": time,
            "median": rt.quantile(0.50, dim=["chain", "draw"]).values,
            "q_low": rt.quantile(q_low, dim=["chain", "draw"]).values,
            "q_high": rt.quantile(q_high, dim=["chain", "draw"]).values,
        }
    )

    plot = (
        p9.ggplot(rt_df, p9.aes(x="day"))
        + p9.geom_ribbon(
            p9.aes(ymin="q_low", ymax="q_high"),
            fill="steelblue",
            alpha=0.25,
        )
        + p9.geom_line(
            p9.aes(y="median"),
            color="darkblue",
            size=1,
        )
        + p9.labs(x="Day", y="Rt", title=title)
    )

    if true_rt is not None:
        true_rt_df = pd.DataFrame({"day": time, "true_Rt": true_rt})
        plot = plot + p9.geom_line(
            data=true_rt_df,
            mapping=p9.aes(x="day", y="true_Rt"),
            color="black",
            linetype="dashed",
            size=0.8,
        )

    return plot
```

```{python}
def load_data(data_dir: Path, signal_name: str = "ed_visits") -> dict:
    """Load synthetic data files and assemble the Stan data dictionary."""
    with open(data_dir / "true_parameters.json") as f:
        params = json.load(f)
    with open(Path(params["signal_config_file"])) as f:
        signal_config = json.load(f)

    signal = next(s for s in signal_config["signals"] if s["name"] == signal_name)
    observations = pl.read_csv(data_dir / params["signal_outputs"][signal_name])

    w: list[float] = params["generation_interval_pmf"]
    pi: list[float] = signal["delay_pmf"]
    S = len(w)
    D = len(pi)
    L = max(D, S)
    T = len(observations)

    return {
        "T": T,
        "S": S,
        "D": D,
        "y": observations["observed_count"].to_list(),
        "w": w,
        "pi": pi,
        "J": params["pre_observation_infections"][-L:],
        "true_Rt": params["true_rt"],
    }
```

```{python}
def viz_data(stan_data: dict):
    """Plot true R(t) and observed counts from a Stan data dictionary."""
    plot_df = pd.DataFrame(
        {
            "time": list(range(1, stan_data["T"] + 1)),
            "Observed count": stan_data["y"],
            "True R(t)": stan_data["true_Rt"],
        }
    ).melt(
        id_vars="time",
        var_name="series",
        value_name="value",
    )

    plot_df["series"] = pd.Categorical(
        plot_df["series"],
        categories=["True R(t)", "Observed count"],
        ordered=True,
    )

    return (
        p9.ggplot(plot_df, p9.aes(x="time", y="value"))
        + p9.geom_line()
        + p9.facet_wrap("~series", ncol=1, scales="free_y")
        + p9.labs(x="Time", y=None)
    )
```

### Load Datasets

Load synthetic data, assemble inputs into data dictionary passed to CmdStanModel's `sample` method.

```{python}
my_data = load_data(Path("data/my_data"))
```

```{python}
viz_data(my_data)
```

```{python}
!ls stan
```

### Run the model

Fit the model, check fit

### Compile the model

Compile the model, show model code

```{python}
STAN_INCLUDE_PATHS = [Path("stan").resolve()]
```

```{python}
ar1_stan_file = Path("stan/simple_diff_ar1_3p.stan")
diff_ar1 = CmdStanModel(stan_file=ar1_stan_file)
```

```{python}
!cat stan/simple_diff_ar1_3p.stan
```

```{python}
cp_stan_file = Path("stan/simple_rw_3p.stan")
cp = CmdStanModel(stan_file=cp_stan_file)
format_stan_file(cp_stan_file, canonicalize="includes", stanc_options={"include-paths" : STAN_INCLUDE_PATHS})
```

```{python}
my_fit = diff_ar1.sample(
        data=my_data,
        chains=4,
        parallel_chains=4,
        seed=42,
        iter_sampling=2000,
    )
```

```{python}
my_time = np.arange(my_data["T"])
my_fit_idata = from_cmdstanpy(
    posterior=my_fit,
    coords={"time": my_time},
    dims={"r": ["time"],"log_r": ["time"]},
    observed_data={
        "ed_visits": np.asarray(my_data["y"]),
        "true_Rt": np.asarray(my_data["true_Rt"]),
    },
)
az.summary(my_fit_idata, var_names=["alpha", "phi", "sigma_rw", "r"])
```

### Visualizations

Plot data and posterior inference to better assess model performance.

```{python}
plot_rt_posterior(my_fit_idata.posterior["r"], true_rt=my_data["true_Rt"])
```
